agent_outputs.raw: The fraud model backtest has three clear gaps for the proposed deployment decision. One: pre-blocked transactions were excluded from training and evaluation. If the model operates only on rules-engine-passed orders in production, this gap is smaller — but needs operational confirmation. Two: the mature-label evaluation window does not cover holiday fraud patterns. Prior holiday seasons with now-mature labels should serve as the held-out test. Three: auto-holds during the holiday season impose asymmetric customer harm (missed deliveries, cancellations, churn) that is not captured by AUC. These three gaps make the backtest insufficient to authorize auto-hold deployment without a shadow phase and harm analysis.

The evaluation is insufficient for the deployment decision as described. The core issue is the mismatch between what was evaluated (historical ranking performance on approved transactions with mature labels) and what is being authorized (automatic holds on live holiday orders). Holiday fraud patterns are not covered by the evaluation. Customer harm from false holds during the peak delivery window is not quantified. Absolute false hold volume at peak order throughput is not estimated. A shadow deployment, prior holiday-season evaluation, and customer harm analysis are all required before activating auto-holds.

Three issues make this backtest insufficient for the auto-hold deployment: (1) the evaluation excluded pre-blocked transactions, potentially misrepresenting the live queue population; (2) the mature-label period excludes holiday-specific fraud; and (3) the customer harm from false holds during the holiday delivery window is unquantified. AUC 0.93 is a strong model ranking signal, but it does not address any of these deployment-specific concerns. Critique wins.

synthesis: All three agents identify the same three core issues and all conclude critique wins. The convergence across three independent assessments is strong. The backtest is a reasonable model development step but is not sufficient for the auto-hold deployment decision without shadow deployment and harm analysis.

etd_constraint.condition: Shadow deployment on current holiday traffic (2-4 weeks, observe-only) plus evaluation on prior holiday season mature labels
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: Shadow false hold rate on legitimate orders exceeds 1%, prior holiday-season evaluation shows materially lower precision than off-season, or customer harm per false hold exceeds operational tolerance
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Shadow false hold rate below 0.5%, holiday-season evaluation matches off-season performance, fraud capture within 5 percentage points of existing controls, customer harm within tolerance
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Performance is acceptable in aggregate but elevated false hold rates appear in specific categories (gift cards, electronics) or customer segments

all_issues_raised: Holiday fraud patterns not in mature-label evaluation (all three agents)
Customer harm from false holds not quantified (all three agents)
